topic: Making Sentences Exciting and Understanding Homophones
title: Adding Extra Details and Learning Homophones

questions.question: What does "Homophones" mean?
options: Words that sound the same but have different spellings and different meanings.
questions.correct_answer: Words that sound the same but have different spellings and different meanings.
questions.explanation: "Homophones" means: Words that sound the same but have different spellings and different meanings.

questions.question: Fill in the blank: Use dashes, commas, or brackets to add ______ extra information to sentences.
questions.explanation: The full sentence is: "Use dashes, commas, or brackets to add exciting extra information to sentences."

questions.question: Which of the following is TRUE about this topic?
options: It has no special meaning in writing.
Use dashes, commas, or brackets to add exciting extra information to sentences.
Always check the spelling of homophones to understand their meaning.
Homophones are words that sound alike but have different spellings and meanings.
questions.correct_answer: Homophones are words that sound alike but have different spellings and meanings.
questions.explanation: Correct! "Homophones are words that sound alike but have different spellings and meanings." is one of the key things to remember.
